result.summary: Runpod exposes a Bearer-authenticated REST API, an API-key-authenticated GraphQL API, two MCP servers (one authenticated, one public), and two CLIs (`flash` and `runpodctl`) that use a Runpod API key.
result.description: Runpod provides GPU compute infrastructure, serverless endpoints, and predeployed AI model endpoints for running and serving AI workloads. Its products include Pods, Serverless, Flash, storage, and public model endpoints.

runpod_api_key.label: Runpod API key
runpod_api_key.setup: In the Runpod console, open [Settings > API Keys](https://www.console.runpod.io/user/settings), choose **Create API Key**, pick permissions, then copy the generated key. The docs note legacy keys existed before Nov 11, 2024, and newer keys can be **All**, **Restricted**, or **Read Only**; [Flash login docs](https://docs.runpod.io/flash/cli/login) say Flash requires an API key with **All** access.

variables.name: endpoint_id
variables.description: Serverless endpoint identifier inserted into the v2 path.

variables.name: model_slug
variables.resolveFrom: the selected Public Endpoint model slug
variables.description: Public model identifier such as `black-forest-labs-flux-1-schnell` used in the v2 path.
